actions.name: Request location
actions.description: Make a one-off request for the user's current location. The user may see a permission prompt which they must approve before any information is returned; if they decline, On error will trigger. Accuracy can be set to High to get more accurate results, but it may take longer to calculate and consume more battery. Timeout is the maximum time in seconds the device may take before it must return a position or trigger On error. Maximum age is the maximum age of a cached result that can be returned. If zero, the device will actively try to determine the user's position at that time. However if it is nonzero, and the operating system had previously requested the user's position within that time, the previous result may be returned immediately instead. This is faster and can save battery, but the result will not be as close to real-time. If a result is successfully determined, On location update will trigger.

actions.name: Watch location
actions.description: As with Request location, but the location will be tracked. On location update will trigger whenever new position information is available, until the Stop watching action is used. Watching the location can consume more battery on mobile devices than one-off requests.

actions.name: Stop watching
actions.description: Stop a previous successful request to watch the user's location. The position will no longer be updated.

conditions.name: Is supported
conditions.description: True if the current device supports reporting the user position with geolocation. If false, none of the features of the object will work.

conditions.name: Is watching location
conditions.description: True after a successful Watch location action, until the Stop watching action is used.

conditions.name: On error
conditions.description: Triggered if an error occurs when requesting permission for, or retrieving, the user's location. The ErrorMessage expression contains more information about the problem in this trigger.

conditions.name: On location update
conditions.description: Triggered after a successful Request location or Watch location action, when the position has been updated. This only triggers once after a successful Request location action, but can trigger regularly after a successful Watch location action whilst the position is tracked and updated.

expressions.name: Accuracy
expressions.description: The estimated accuracy in meters of the latitude and longitude (for Accuracy) or the altitude (for AltitudeAccuracy). The accuracy may be more or less a guess, or if it is not known the expression returns 0.

expressions.name: AltitudeAccuracy
expressions.description: The estimated altitude in meters relative to sea-level, or 0 if not known.

expressions.name: Altitude
expressions.description: In On error, a string with some additional information about the error.

expressions.name: ErrorMessage
expressions.description: While watching a position, the direction of travel in degrees relative to due north and speed in meters per second if available, else 0 of not available.

expressions.name: Heading
expressions.description: The latitude and longitude that has been determined, subject to the Accuracy (which may not be known).

expressions.name: Speed
expressions.description: A timestamp of the time at which the current details were retrieved. This is measured in milliseconds since midnight, January 1, 1970 (also known as a UNIX timestamp).
